member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.AsyncResultCompletion
member.summary:
Used with
AsyncResultNoResult.SetAsCompleted and
AsyncResult<TResult>.SetAsCompleted.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.AsyncResultCompletion.IsSync
member.summary: Equivalent to true for the #x201C;completedSynchronously” parameter.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.AsyncResultCompletion.IsAsync
member.summary: Equivalent to false for the #x201C;completedSynchronously” parameter.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.AsyncResultCompletion.MakeAsync
member.summary: Forces the callback to run on a thread-pool thread.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.GuidHelper
member.summary: Contains logic to switch byte ordering from host to network (Big-endian). If the host is big-endian then no reording is done.
member.remarks: This class is modelled on IPAddress.HostToNetworkOrder which is used for integer types.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.GuidHelper.HostToNetworkOrder(System.Guid)
member.summary: Converts a value from host byte order to network byte order.
member.param: The to convert, expressed in host byte order.
member.returns: A value, expressed in network byte order.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.GuidHelper.NetworkToHostOrder(System.Guid)
member.summary: Converts a value from network byte order to host byte order.
member.param: The to convert, expressed in network byte order.
member.returns: A value, expressed in host byte order.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames
member.summary: Specifies the media type information for an object.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Text
member.summary: Specifies the type of text data in an object.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Text.vCalendar
member.summary: Specifies that the data is in vCalendar format.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Text.vCard
member.summary: Specifies that the data is in vCard format.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Text.vMessage
member.summary: Specifies that the data is in vMsg format.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Text.vNote
member.summary: Specifies that the data is in vNote format.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Text.vToDo
member.summary: Specifies that the data is in vToDo format.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.ObjectExchange
member.summary: Specifies the type of Object Exchange specific data.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.ObjectExchange.Capabilities
member.summary: Used to retrieve supported object types.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.ObjectExchange.FolderListing
member.summary: Used to retrieve folder listing with OBEX FTP.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.ObjectExchange.ObjectProfile
member.summary: Used to retrieve an object profile.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Messaging
member.summary: Specifies the type of Messaging Access Profile (MAP) specific data.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Messaging.Message
member.summary: Used to retrieve a text message.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Messaging.MessageListing
member.summary: Used to retrieve message listing with MAP.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.Messaging.EventReport
member.summary: Used to report events.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.PhoneBookAccess
member.summary: Specifies the type of Phone Book Access Profile (PBAP) specific data.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.PhoneBookAccess.PhoneBook
member.summary: Used to retrieve a phone book. This is a collection of vCard items with custom fields.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.PhoneBookAccess.VCard
member.summary: Used to retrieve an individual vCard entry.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Mime.MediaTypeNames.PhoneBookAccess.VCardListing
member.summary: Used to retrieve a vCard listing from a query.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader
member.summary: Header identifiers to describe some aspect of the object being transferred.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Name
member.summary: String describing the name of the object.
member.remarks: Unicode String. Often a file name.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Description
member.summary: Text description of the object.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Type
member.summary: Type of object - e.g. text, html, binary, manufacturer specific
member.remarks: Byte sequence. ASCII encoded MIME defined media type.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Time
member.summary: Date/time stamp – ISO 8601 version - preferred
member.remarks: Byte sequence. ASCII encoded ISO 8601 DateTime string.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Target
member.summary: Name of service that operation is targeted to.
member.remarks: Byte sequence. Usually a Uuid for a well-known target.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Http
member.summary: An HTTP 1.x header.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Body
member.summary: A chunk of the object body.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.EndOfBody
member.summary: The final chunk of the object body.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Who
member.summary: Identifies the OBEX application, used to tell if talking to a peer.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.AuthChallenge
member.summary: Authentication digest-challenge.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.AuthResponse
member.summary: Authentication digest-response.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.ObjectClassOld
member.summary: OBEX Object class of object.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.WanUuid
member.summary: Uniquely identifies the network client (OBEX server).

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.ObjectClass
member.summary: OBEX Object class of object.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.SessionParameters
member.summary: Parameters used in session commands/responses.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.SessionSequenceNumber
member.summary: Sequence number used in each OBEX packet for reliability.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Count
member.summary: Number of objects (used by Connect).

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Length
member.summary: The length of the object in bytes.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.Time4Byte
member.summary: Date/time stamp – 4-byte version.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.ConnectionID
member.summary: An identifier used for OBEX connection multiplexing.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexHeader.CreatorID
member.summary: Indicates the creator of an object.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexListener
member.summary: Provides a simple, programmatically controlled OBEX protocol listener.

members.member:
Initializes a new instance of the ObexListener class.
-
Initializes a new instance of the ObexListener class using the Bluetooth transport.

Initializes a new instance of the ObexListener class specifiying the transport to use.
- Specifies the transport protocol to use.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListener.IsListening
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the has been started.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexListener.Start
member.summary: Allows this instance to receive incoming requests.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexListener.Stop
member.summary: Causes this instance to stop receiving incoming requests.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexListener.Close
member.summary: Shuts down the ObexListener.

members.member:
Waits for an incoming request and returns when one is received.
-
This method blocks waiting for a new connection. It will return when a new connection completes or / has been called.
- Returns a or if / has been called.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerContext
member.summary: Provides access to the request and response objects used by the class.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerContext.Request
member.summary: Gets the that represents a client's request for a resource

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest
member.summary: Describes an incoming OBEX request to an object.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.ContentLength64
member.summary:
Gets the length of the body data included in the request.
New in v1.5.51015
member.value: A long value that contains the value from the request's Length header. This value is -1 if the content length is not known.
member.remarks: The Length header expresses the length, in bytes, of the body data that accompanies the request.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.ContentType
member.summary: Gets the MIME type of the body data included in the request.
member.value: A that contains the text of the request's Type header.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.Headers
member.summary: Gets the collection of header name/value pairs sent in the request.
member.value: A that contains the OBEX headers included in the request.
member.remarks: For a complete list of request headers, see the enumeration.

members.member:
Get the device address and service to which the request is directed.
-
The instance returned will be of the subtype that matches the address family that the is listening on. For instance if the listener was created with . then the will be of type , and similarly for and .
-

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.ObexMethod
member.summary: Gets the method specified by the client.
member.remarks: Only PUT is supported in this version.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.InputStream
member.summary: Gets a stream that contains the body data sent by the client.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.ProtocolVersion
member.summary: Gets the OBEX version used by the requesting client

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.RawUrl
member.summary: Gets the URL information (without the host and port) requested by the client.
member.value: A that contains the raw URL for this request.

members.member:
Gets the device address and service from which the request originated.
-
The instance returned will be of the subtype that matches the address family that the is listening on. For instance if the listener was created with . then the will be of type , and similarly for and .
-
C#
ObexListener lsnr = new ObexListener(ObexTransport.Bluetooth) ... ... ObexListenerRequest olr = ... BluetoothEndPoint remoteEp = (BluetoothEndPoint)olr.RemoteEndPoint; BluetoothAddress remoteAddr = remoteEp.Address;
Visual Basic
Dim lsnr As New ObexListener(ObexTransport.IrDA) ... ... Dim olr As ObexListenerRequest = ... Dim remoteEp As IrDAEndPoint = CType(olr.RemoteEndPoint, IrDAEndPoint); Dim remoteAddr As IrDAAddress = remoteEp.Address;
-

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.UserHostAddress
member.summary: Gets the server address to which the request is directed.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.Url
member.summary: Gets the object requested by the client.
member.value: A object that identifies the resource requested by the client.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexListenerRequest.WriteFile(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the body of the request to the specified file path.
member.param: The filename (including the path) to write to.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod
member.summary: Methods which can be carried out in an Object Exchange transaction.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.Put
member.summary: Sends an object to a receiving device.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.Get
member.summary: Requests a file from the remote device.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.Connect
member.summary: Negotiate an Object Exchange connection with a remote device.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.Disconnect
member.summary: Disconnect an existing Object Exchange session.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.PutFinal
member.summary: Sends the last packet of an object to a receiving device.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.SetPath
member.summary: Change remote path on an Object Exchange server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.Session
member.summary: Used for reliable session support.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexMethod.Abort
member.summary: Abort the current operation.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode
member.summary: Specifies the status codes returned for an Object Exchange (OBEX) operation.
member.remarks: OBEX codes are directly related to their HTTP equivalents - see .

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Final
member.summary: Applied to another code to indicate this is the only response or final response in a series.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Continue
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 100. Continue indicates that the client can continue with its request.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.OK
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 200. OK indicates that the request succeeded and that the requested information is in the response. This is the most common status code to receive.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Created
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 201. Created indicates that the request resulted in a new resource created before the response was sent.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Accepted
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 202. Accepted indicates that the request has been accepted for further processing.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.NonAuthorativeInformation
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 203. NonAuthoritativeInformation indicates that the returned metainformation is from a cached copy instead of the origin server and therefore may be incorrect.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.NoContent
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 204. NoContent indicates that the request has been successfully processed and that the response is intentionally blank.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.ResetContent
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 205. ResetContent indicates that the client should reset (not reload) the current resource.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.PartialContent
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 206. PartialContent indicates that the response is a partial response as requested by a GET request that includes a byte range.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.MultipleChoices
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 300. MultipleChoices indicates that the requested information has multiple representations.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.MovedPermanently
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 301. MovedPermanently indicates that the requested information has been moved to the URI specified in the Location header. The default action when this status is received is to follow the Location header associated with the response.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.MovedTemporarily
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 302. Redirect indicates that the requested information is located at the URI specified in the Location header. The default action when this status is received is to follow the Location header associated with the response. When the original request method was POST, the redirected request will use the GET method.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.SeeOther
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 303. SeeOther automatically redirects the client to the URI specified in the Location header as the result of a POST. The request to the resource specified by the Location header will be made with a GET.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.NotModified
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 304. NotModified indicates that the client's cached copy is up to date. The contents of the resource are not transferred.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.UseProxy
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 305. UseProxy indicates that the request should use the proxy server at the URI specified in the Location header.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.BadRequest
member.summary:
Equivalent to HTTP status 400. BadRequest indicates that the request could not be understood by the server. BadRequest is sent when no other error is applicable, or if the exact error is unknown or does not have its own error code.
reports errors through ObexWebResponse.StatusCode, this status code is overloaded by it to report failure to connect to the server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Unauthorized
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 401. Unauthorized indicates that the requested resource requires authentication. The WWW-Authenticate header contains the details of how to perform the authentication.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.PaymentRequired
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 402. PaymentRequired is reserved for future use.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Forbidden
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 403. Forbidden indicates that the server refuses to fulfill the request.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.NotFound
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 404. NotFound indicates that the requested resource does not exist on the server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.MethodNotAllowed
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 405. MethodNotAllowed indicates that the request method (POST or GET) is not allowed on the requested resource.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.NotAcceptable
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 406. NotAcceptable indicates that the client has indicated with Accept headers that it will not accept any of the available representations of the resource.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.ProxyAuthenticationRequired
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 407. ProxyAuthenticationRequired indicates that the requested proxy requires authentication. The Proxy-authenticate header contains the details of how to perform the authentication.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.RequestTimeout
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 408. RequestTimeout indicates that the client did not send a request within the time the server was expecting the request.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Conflict
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 409. Conflict indicates that the request could not be carried out because of a conflict on the server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.Gone
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 410. Gone indicates that the requested resource is no longer available.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.LengthRequired
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 411. LengthRequired indicates that the required Content-length header is missing.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.PreconditionFailed
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 412. PreconditionFailed indicates that a condition set for this request failed, and the request cannot be carried out. Conditions are set with conditional request headers like If-Match, If-None-Match, or If-Unmodified-Since.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.RequestedEntityTooLarge
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 413. RequestEntityTooLarge indicates that the request is too large for the server to process.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.RequestedUrlTooLarge
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 414. RequestUriTooLong indicates that the URI is too long.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.UnsupportedMediaType
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 415. UnsupportedMediaType indicates that the request is an unsupported type.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.InternalServerError
member.summary:
Equivalent to HTTP status 500. InternalServerError indicates that a generic error has occurred on the server.
reports errors through ObexWebResponse.StatusCode, this status code is used by it to report failure to send the object.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.NotImplemented
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 501. NotImplemented indicates that the server does not support the requested function.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.BadGateway
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 502. BadGateway indicates that an intermediate proxy server received a bad response from another proxy or the origin server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.ServiceUnavailable
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 503. ServiceUnavailable indicates that the server is temporarily unavailable, usually due to high load or maintenance.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.GatewayTimeout
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 504. GatewayTimeout indicates that an intermediate proxy server timed out while waiting for a response from another proxy or the origin server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.HttpVersionNotSupported
member.summary: Equivalent to HTTP status 505. HttpVersionNotSupported indicates that the requested HTTP version is not supported by the server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.DatabaseFull
member.summary: Database Full.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexStatusCode.DatabaseLocked
member.summary: Database Locked.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexTarget
member.summary: Known Target header values for Bluetooth defined services.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexTarget.MessagingAccess
member.summary: Target header to connect to Messaging Access Service (Part of Messaging Access Profile).

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexTarget.MessagingNotification
member.summary: Target header to connect to Messaging Notification Service (Part of Messaging Access Profile).

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexTarget.PhoneBookAccess
member.summary: Target header to connect to Phone Book Access Profile.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexTransport
member.summary: Supported network transports for Object Exchange.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexTransport.IrDA
member.summary: Infrared (IrDA)

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexTransport.Bluetooth
member.summary: Bluetooth

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.ObexTransport.Tcp
member.summary: TCP/IP

members.member:
Provides an OBEX implementation of the class.
-
If you want to transfer a file or other object using the standard service as used by Windows' Wireless Link / Bluetooth File Transfer Wizard, Palm's Beam, Nokia's Send via Infrared, then use the OBEX protocol.
The PUT operation is supported, and there is new support for GET, (see the documentation at the property). Changing folders is not supported, nor is getting a folder listing.
In the previous version there were some issue with handling file names that include non-English characters, and connections to some device types failed. Also if the connection to the peer was lost then the request could hang reading forever. See the release note and bugs database for more information.
-
For Bluetooth one can use code like the following to send a file: (Note a failure is signalled by an exception).
Dim addr As BluetoothAddress = BluetoothAddress.Parse("002233445566") Dim path As String = "HelloWorld.txt" ' Dim req As New ObexWebRequest(addr, path) req.ReadFile("Hello World.txt") Dim rsp As ObexWebResponse = CType(req.GetResponse(),ObexWebResponse) Console.WriteLine("Response Code: {0} (0x{0:X})", rsp.StatusCode)
That constructor isn't available for other transports (TCP/IP, IrDA) so one has to create a Uri to provide the scheme, address, and path parameters. Thus use code like the following to send a file.
' The host part of the URI is the device address, e.g. IrDAAddress.ToString(), ' and the file part is the OBEX object name. Dim addr As BluetoothAddress = ... Dim addrStr As String = addr.ToString("N") Dim uri As New Uri("obex://" & addrStr & "/HelloWorld.txt") ' Dim req As New ObexWebRequest(uri) req.ReadFile("Hello World.txt") Dim rsp As ObexWebResponse = CType(req.GetResponse(),ObexWebResponse) Console.WriteLine("Response Code: {0} (0x{0:X})", rsp.StatusCode)
Or, to send locally generated content use something like the following.
Dim addr As BluetoothAddress = ... Dim path As String = "HelloWorld2.txt" ' Dim req As New ObexWebRequest(addr, path) Using content As Stream = req.GetRequestStream() ' Using a StreamWriter to write text to the stream... Using wtr As New StreamWriter(content) wtr.WriteLine("Hello World GetRequestStream") wtr.WriteLine("Hello World GetRequestStream 2") wtr.Flush() ' Set the Length header value req.ContentLength = content.Length End Using ' In this case closing the StreamWriter also closed the Stream, but ... End Using Dim rsp As ObexWebResponse = CType(req.GetResponse(),ObexWebResponse) Console.WriteLine("Response Code: {0} (0x{0:X})", rsp.StatusCode)
See also the ObexPushApplication and ObexPushVB sample programs.

Create a new Obex request with the specified .
-
Create a new Obex request with the specified .
e.g. "obex://112233445566/HelloWorld.txt" Uri must use one of the following schemes - obex, obex-push, obex-ftp, obex-sync. The host name must be the device address in short hex, or dotted hex notation - not the default representation using the colon separator

[Advanced usage] Create a new Obex request with the specified and the open connection to an OBEX server.
- [Advanced usage] A url of the form “scheme:///filename”, “e.g. obex:///foo.txt”. That is the host part is blank, and the scheme and filename parts set as for the other constructor
An instance of already connected to an OBEX server.

Initialize an instance of this class given a scheme, a Bluetooth Device Address, and a remote path name.
- The Uri scheme. One of obex, obex-push, obex-ftp, or obex-sync.
The Bluetooth Device Address of the OBEX server.
The path on the OBEX server.

Initialize an instance of this class given a Bluetooth Device Address, and a remote path name.
-
This is equivalent to calling
ObexWebRequest(String scheme, BluetoothAddress target, String path)
with scheme “obex”.
- The Bluetooth Device Address of the OBEX server.
The path on the OBEX server.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.Headers
member.summary: Specifies a collection of the name/value pairs that make up the OBEX headers.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.Method
member.summary: Gets or sets the method for the request.

remarks.para: For Object Exchange the method code is mapped to the equivalent HTTP style method. For example "PUT", "GET" etc. "PUT" is the default value. There is new support for GET as of version 2.5.
To use GET change the Method to "GET" and you must also use scheme "obex-ftp" in the URL instead of the usual "obex" -- unless you know that the default OBEX server you are connecting supports GET.
For a PUT sample see the class documentation. For GET, see below.

example.code: ' The host part of the URI is the device address, e.g. IrDAAddress.ToString(), ' and the file part is the OBEX object name. Dim addr As String = "112233445566" Dim uri As New Uri("obex-ftp://" & addr & "/HelloWorld.txt") Dim req As New ObexWebRequest(uri) req.Method = "GET" Dim rsp As ObexWebResponse = CType(req.GetResponse(), ObexWebResponse) Console.WriteLine("Response Code: {0} (0x{0:X})", rsp.StatusCode) Using content As Stream = rsp.GetResponseStream() ' Using a StreamReader to read text from the stream... Using rdr As New StreamReader(content) While True Dim line As String = rdr.ReadLine() If line Is Nothing Then Exit While Console.WriteLine(line) End While End Using End Using

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.ContentType
member.summary: Gets or sets the value of the Type OBEX header.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.ContentLength
member.summary: Gets or sets the Length OBEX header.
member.remarks: This property is mandatory, if not set no data will be sent. If you use the helper method this value is automatically populated with the size of the file that was read.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.Proxy
member.summary: Not Supported - do not use, this will throw an exception.

members.member:
Gets or sets the time-out value for the method.
-
In versions 3.2 and earlier this property was ignored on Windows Mobile. It is now (untested!) supported there, but not with the Microsoft Bluetooth stack there as it doesn't support timeouts. A cunning solution is available let me know of your requirements...
- The number of milliseconds to wait before the request times out. The default is 50,000 milliseconds (50 seconds). A value of -1 or 0 represents no time-out.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.RequestUri
member.summary: Gets the original Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) of the request.
member.remarks: For an ObexPush request the URI will use the "obex://" prefix, followed by the numerical device id in hex format. The path section of the URI represents the remote filename of the pushed object. Subfolders are not supported. Some devices may only support specific object types e.g. V-Card.

members.member:
Gets a object to use to write request data.
- A to use to write request data.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.ReadFile(System.String)
member.summary: Reads the contents of the specified file to the request stream.
member.param: The filename (including the path) from which to read.
member.remarks: Provides an easy equivalent to manually writing the file contents to the request stream.

members.member:
Returns the OBEX server response.
- An . -
An error occurred, with the error that occured being stored in the property. If the error occurred in the connect phase then the property will have value , and in the operation phase on the desktop CLR it will have value

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.StreamReadBlockMust(System.IO.Stream,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: A wrapper for Stream.Read that blocks until the requested number of bytes have been read, and throw an exception if the stream is closed before that occurs.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequest.StreamReadBlock(System.IO.Stream,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: A wrapper for Stream.Read that blocks until the requested number of bytes have been read or the end of the Stream has been reached. Returns the number of bytes read.

members.member:
Begins a request for a OBEX server response.
- An delegate that references the method to invoke when the operation is complete. A user-defined object containing information about the operation. This object is passed to the callback delegate when the operation is complete. - An that represents the asynchronous operation, which could still be pending.

Begins a request for a OBEX server response.
- An object that was obtained when the asynchronous operation was started.
- An . -
An error occurred, with the error that occured being stored in the property. If the error occurred in the connect phase then the property will have value , and in the operation phase on the desktop CLR it will have value

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebRequestCreate
member.summary: Used to create a new web request for obex uri scheme

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebResponse
member.summary: Provides an OBEX implementation of the class.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebResponse.Headers
member.summary: Gets the headers associated with this response from the server.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebResponse.ContentLength
member.summary: Gets the length of the content returned by the request.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebResponse.ContentType
member.summary: Gets the content type of the response.

members.member:
Returns a status code to indicate the outcome of the request.
-
Note, if a error occurs locally then the status code is returned. Therefore that error code could signal local or remote errors.

Gets the stream used to read the body of the response from the server.
- A containing the body of the response.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.ObexWebResponse.Close
member.summary: Frees the resources held by the response.
